danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct race-specific unit sprites and armor designs
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates hostile faction activity and military threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Map overlay showing enemy-controlled regions with faction color coding
danger_signals.meaning: Signals loss of resource nodes and strategic position compromise

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated depletion of stone, wood, and ore deposits in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates economic vulnerability and supply chain breakdown risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Damaged or burnt building sprites with animation states
danger_signals.meaning: Communicates active combat engagement and infrastructure damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Workers moving between buildings with resource carrying animations
safety_signals.meaning: Indicates stable economy and operational infrastructure

safety_signals.visual_form: Soldiers standing at attention in garrison buildings, armed with faction-specific weapons
safety_signals.meaning: Signals defensive preparedness and deterrent presence

safety_signals.visual_form: Settler animations and increased unit density in settlements
safety_signals.meaning: Communicates expanding workforce and economic capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Map region highlighted in player's faction color with buildings present
safety_signals.meaning: Indicates secure resource access and stable dominion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Roman imperial aesthetic with distinctive architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: When controlled by AI, same unit sprites but contextually hostile
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Roman-style fortifications, rectangular building layouts, classical architectural elements

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct unit armor and weapon designs differentiating from Roman troops
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Culturally distinct building designs and settlement patterns

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Northern-themed armor and weaponry with visual distinctiveness
Visually distinct unit designs with alternative weapon types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Asian-inspired architectural style and building arrangement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Combat animations between unit sprites, building damage sprites showing progression, smoke/fire effects at conflict locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units moving toward enemy positions, defenders engaging attackers, casualty animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clear map areas with settler construction animations, stable building structures, uninterrupted resource gathering
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Workers methodically moving between production chains, construction activity at new building sites, scouts exploring unclaimed territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Depleted resource nodes with visual indicators, idle workers standing in settlements, partially constructed buildings in stalled state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Settlers waiting at resource sites, slowed movement animations, reduced construction activity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy territory visibly diminished on map, player territory expanded with dense building clusters, objective location heavily fortified
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Concentrated military unit groupings, aggressive patrol patterns, defensive positioning around target area

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Magical portals as environmental story markers, small initial settlement footprint, vast unexplored territory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited worker availability, gradual population growth, exploration-focused settler movements

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of four races constructs buildings with culturally-distinct aesthetics (Roman classical, Nubian African, Viking Nordic, Japanese Asian), making faction presence immediately readable on the map without text labels
Map overlay displays faction-controlled regions in faction colors, allowing player to read geopolitical relationships and territorial disputes at a glance
Stone, wood, and ore deposits are visually distinct on terrain, with animated depletion animations showing economic impact and encouraging strategic harvesting
Campaign story progresses through interaction with magical portals, which serve as visual story progression devices and environmental world-building elements
Concentrated building clusters visually communicate economic strength and territorial control density, with sparse settlements indicating weak or newly conquered regions
Frequent settler animations with varied movements and interactions communicate active, functioning economy versus idle, struggling economies with stationary workers

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Animated movement between buildings carrying resources, consistent walking patterns following supply chain routes
Standing still in settlement, no carrying animation, occasionally looking around waiting for job assignment
Standing at attention within fortification building, armed, stationary position indicating readiness
Movement toward enemy units with attack animations, collision detection with enemy sprites triggering combat sequences
Regular walking movements across controlled map regions in predictable patrol patterns
Rapid movement into fog-of-war territory, revealing map areas, moving ahead of main army
Animation indicating active production, workers entering/exiting, smoke/activity effects
Static sprite with no animation activity, workers standing idle inside or nearby, no resource output visible

notes: The Settlers II uses a classic RTS visual communication system where faction identity is primarily conveyed through architectural style and color coding rather than symbolic sigils. The game's strength in visual world-state communication lies in settlement density visualization, animated worker activity as economic health indicator, and clear territorial color segmentation. The four aesthetically distinct races create immediate visual faction recognition. Campaign narrative is supported through magical portal environmental markers. Combat presence is communicated through unit sprite visibility and animation states. Economic state (success/scarcity) is readable through worker movement density and resource depletion animation. The game emphasizes supply-chain animation as a core communication method—active, flowing worker movements signal healthy economy while stationary, idle workers immediately communicate economic crisis.
